b:Ta6fa,<div class="specification">
<p>When water rises above a certain temperature, coral bleaching may occur. The coral expels <em>Zooxanthellae</em> algae living in its tissues, leaving the coral a white colour. The graph shows how the water temperature is changing in the coral reefs surrounding the Cayman Islands.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">State the trend in temperatures from 1982 to 2014. </p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Deduce the effect of bleaching on corals. </p>
<div class="marks">[4]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>c:T4e0,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">«overall» water temperature increasing<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>the range of fluctuations increases ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;"><em>Do not accept the trend is fluctuating</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">a. the coral and the algae have a symbiotic/mutualistic relationship<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>two different organisms live together and depend on each other ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">b. algae expelled from coral/relationship broken ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">c. coral produces carbon dioxide from respiration ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">d. the algae use the carbon dioxide to carry out photosynthesis/produce nutrients ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">e. without the nutrients from the algae the coral are liable to starve ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">f. coral dies/is susceptible to disease ✔</p>
<p style="text-align: left;"> </p>
<p style="text-align: left;"><em>Do not accept effects on species other than coral</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/c/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","c"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"18N.3.SL.TZ0.15"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"18N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","c-1-species-and-communities"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
